description: Hand-curated narrative companion to `gohealthcli describe-schema --json`. Merged into the live catalog at command invocation time; downstream tools (Claude skill, MCP server, dashboard) can also fetch this file directly without running the binary.
guidance: For unit-aware queries, JOIN current_settings on connection_id to get the user's measurement system (METRIC | IMPERIAL) and timezone.
For free-text needles like a device model or app name, query searchable_text WHERE text LIKE '%needle%' instead of guessing which underlying column to read.
Raw Data Points stay the source of truth â€” Normalized Views recompute on read. Never write to a view.
Sleep Data Points (record_kind='session') carry the session interval at the top level and an optional `sleep.stages` array projected through sleep_stages.
Exercise distance is exposed via exercise_splits in BOTH meters (distance_meters) and millimeters (distance_millimeters). Use millimeters when sub-meter precision matters.

data_points.purpose: Every raw Data Point archived from the Google Health provider. Always JOIN on connection_id and filter by data_type.

columns.raw_json: The verbatim provider response; query via json_extract paths. The Normalized Views project the most-asked-for fields.
columns.record_kind: One of `interval`, `sample`, `daily`, or `session`. Different parsers; different time fields populated.
columns.source_family_filter: Empty string when the row came from a default `list` sync; `wearable` when sourced via `reconcile` with a source-family filter.

rollups.purpose: Provider-computed aggregates (daily, hourly, custom-window). Use only when you need provider's own summary instead of computing from data_points.

columns.rollup_kind: Provider endpoint family that produced the row. `dailyRollUp` is what `sync --rollup daily` writes.

identity_snapshots.purpose: Kind-tagged identity-level metadata (profile, settings, paired-devices, irn-profile). Append-only; query through the current_* views or paired_devices instead of joining raw rows.

sync_runs.purpose: Audit trail of every sync attempt. `status` âˆˆ {sync_running, sync_completed, sync_failed, sync_canceled}. Don't use this as a resume cursor â€” that's sync_cursors.

sync_cursors.purpose: Durable resume highwater per (connection, data_type, source_family_filter, rollup_kind). Advances only on sync_completed (ADR-0008).
